steps.description: Simple mixing of one part cationic dye (Rhodamine 3B perchlorate) with two parts cyanostar macrocycle to produce SMILES materials. The dye's counter anions (ClO4-) form complexes with cyanostar, and the resulting disc-like 2:1 cyanostar-anion complexes direct crystal packing into an alternating charge-by-charge assembly.

Co-crystallization of the mixture produces SMILES crystals with alternating charge-by-charge packing of cationic dyes with anionic complexes. Crystals of various sizes (~2 µm to ~50 µm) were produced.

synthesis.notes: SMILES (small-molecule ionic isolation lattices) materials are prepared by a simple two-pot mix: one part cationic dye with two parts cyanostar. The cyanostar macrocycle binds the perchlorate counter anion of rhodamine 3B, forming a 2:1 cyanostar-anion complex that directs alternating ionic packing. The resulting crystals show CCDC code 1892437. Thin films can also be prepared by spin-coating. The design requires: (1) cationic dyes, (2) macrocycles with strong anion affinities (cyanostar), and (3) complementary anions (ClO4-, BF4-, PF6-). Electronic isolation is achieved because the dye's HOMO and LUMO levels reside inside the wide 3.45-eV band gap of the cyanostar-anion complex. SMILES crystals of R3B show quantum yield of 29% compared to 3% for R3B·ClO4 crystals alone.

steps.description: One part cationic styryl dye (pyridine 1·ClO4) is mixed with two equivalents of cyanostar macrocycle. The cyanostar binds the perchlorate counter anion, forming a 2:1 cyanostar-anion complex that directs ionic crystallization into an alternating charge-by-charge packing arrangement.

The mixture undergoes ionic co-crystallization, producing SMILES crystals with alternating cationic dye and anionic cyanostar-perchlorate complex packing (CCDC 1892439). The resulting crystals show reinstated emission and solution-like optical properties.

synthesis.notes: SMILES (small-molecule ionic isolation lattices) are formed by a simple two-pot mix of one part cationic dye with two parts cyanostar. The cyanostar macrocycle binds the ClO4- counter anion to form a 2:1 disc-like anionic complex that directs alternating charge-by-charge crystal packing, providing both spatial isolation (dye-dye distances 14-18 Å along stacking axis) and electronic isolation (dye HOMO/LUMO nested within the 3.45-eV band gap of the cyanostar-anion complex). Crystal structure deposited as CCDC 1892439. Thin films can also be prepared by spin-coating from solution.

steps.description: Two equivalents of cyanostar macrocycle are mixed with one equivalent of the cyanine dye DIOC2·PF6. The cyanostar macrocycles bind the PF6- counter anions, forming 2:1 cyanostar-anion complexes that direct alternating charge-by-charge packing of cationic dyes and anionic complexes.

The mixture undergoes ionic crystallization to form SMILES crystals (CCDC 1892440) with alternating charge-by-charge packing of cationic dye and anionic cyanostar-PF6- complexes, spatially and electronically isolating the fluorophores.
